name: Horizontes: LatAm Business Leadership Insights
description: Horizontes helps Latin American business leaders understand key issues affecting their companies/industries and further develop their leadership skills via in-depth talks with a wide range of expert guests.

recent_episodes.title: The Shifting Commercial and Industrial Real Estate Markets in Latin America
recent_episodes.description:
Send us a text

COVID proved to be a pivotal moment in Latin America's real-estate market.  A region once characterized as having a healthy commercial real-estate market and a fledging industrial real-estate market was completely upended by the pandemic.  Now it seams that a new normal is taking shape.

We spoke with Juan Gallardo, EVP, Latin America, for the real estate giant Colliers, to learn more about this new normal and what's next.

recent_episodes.title: US responds to Chinese engagement in Latin America
recent_episodes.description:
Send us a text

Dr. Robert-Evan Ellis returns to the Horizontes podcast to update us on the status of US-China competition in Latin America.  The Trump administration is far more explicit than previous US administrations at identifying the threat of Chinese commercial, diplomatic and military engagement in Latin America.  But are US tactics the right formula to countering the China threat?  And how will Latin American leaders navigate their futures under the shadow of a growing US-China

recent_episodes.title: US-Latin America foreign policy braces for disruption: A conversation with Eduardo Gamarra of Florida International University
recent_episodes.description:
Send us a text

The world awaits with bated breath to learn how Donald Trump and Marco Rubio plan to reshape US foreign relations. No region will be as profoundly impacted as Latin America, as the US reconsiders long-standing positions on trade, migration, aid and military cooperation. To help predict some of those policy changes as well as the far reaching influence of Trump's unique brand of politics, we turned to Dr. Eduardo Gamarra of Florida International University (FIU). score_reason: Features business leadership discussions with expert guests, though focus on LatAm may limit fit unless Andreas has regional expertise or insights.

key_themes: Disruption and transformation in traditional industries
Leadership decision-making under uncertainty and change
Technology adoption and competitive advantage in Latin America
enrichment.relevant_episode: Latin America 10 Years into the Future — This episode directly addresses how disruptive technology is changing competitive landscapes and forcing companies to rethink product portfolios, which aligns perfectly with Andreas's expertise in helping traditional industries use AI to compete.
enrichment.coverage_gap: How artificial intelligence and data-driven decision-making specifically apply to Latin American agri-food and commodity businesses—the podcast has covered energy transition, logistics, and healthcare but hasn't addressed AI-powered transformation in agriculture or food systems, which are core to the region's economy.
enrichment.suggested_hook: The executive who built and sold Canada's largest food agency now teaches AI strategy to MBAs—here's what Latin American agricultural and food leaders need to know to stop being afraid of AI and start using it to outcompete.
